ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a branded multi-component fast food sharing meal set. FOODON_00002473 (fast food meal) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Fast foods) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a branded multi-component sharing meal set from Jollibee.

serving.description: 1 sharing meal set for two persons (estimated total weight ~900–1100 g across all components)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Value for Two Meal B (Dinner) set at Jollibee Singapore, comprising approximately two servings of fried chicken, a burger, sides such as fries or rice, and two drinks, with a total estimated weight of approximately 1000 g shared between two diners.

nutrient_source.title: Jollibee Philippines Nutrition Information — Chickenjoy, Yumburger, Jolly Spaghetti, Fries, and Drinks; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central composite fast food meal estimates
nutrient_source.organization: Jollibee Foods Corporation (official nutrition disclosure) and USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The 'Value for Two Meal B (Dinner)' is a bundled sharing set sold at Jollibee Singapore (Bugis Village). The exact Singapore-specific bundle composition is not published in a primary nutrition database. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by constructing a representative composite of typical Jollibee Meal B dinner components: two pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken (~280 g), one Yumburger (~120 g), one regular Jolly Crispy Fries (~115 g), one serving of steamed rice (~180 g), and two regular soft drinks (~500 g combined), totalling approximately 1195 g. Per-100 g values are derived by dividing total estimated nutrient content by 11.95. Jollibee Philippines official nutrition data was used as the primary component reference, supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken (NDB 05062), fast food burger (NDB 21108), french fries (NDB 21137), and cooked white rice (NDB 20045). Confidence is medium due to bundle composition uncertainty and Singapore-specific portion variation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — composite fast food meal with fried chicken, white rice, burger bun, and fries analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Value for Two Meal B (Dinner) bundle. GI is estimated as a weighted composite of the meal's primary glycemic carbohydrate contributors: steamed white rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney), white burger bun (GI ~70, University of Sydney), and french fries (GI ~63, University of Sydney). Soft drinks contribute simple sugars (GI ~63 for sucrose-based beverages). Fried chicken and condiments contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrates. Weighted composite GI is estimated at approximately 62 (medium category). Glycemic load per serving is calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (62 × 48.5 g carbohydrate in a 200 g per-person portion) / 100 ≈ 30.1, rounded to 30.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~390 mg per 100 g; estimated ~3900 mg per full meal set for two)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal set, as the estimated total sodium content of approximately 3900 mg per full set far exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg, primarily driven by fried chicken seasoning, burger sauces, and condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~30 GL per person per sitting) from white rice, burger bun, fries, and sugar-sweetened beverages
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal set due to its high glycemic load of approximately 30 per person per sitting, driven by refined carbohydrates in white rice, burger buns, and french fries combined with sugar-sweetened soft drinks, which will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and caloric density from deep-fried chicken and processed burger components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal set, as the combination of deep-fried chicken (high saturated and trans fat), processed burger, sodium-rich condiments, and calorie-dense sides contributes to atherogenic lipid profiles and elevated blood pressure risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Value for Two Meal B (Dinner) is a bundled fast food sharing set sold at Jollibee Singapore, specifically at the Bugis Village outlet, priced at SGD 29.60. Jollibee is a Filipino-origin fast food chain known for its signature Chickenjoy fried chicken, Yumburger, and Jolly Spaghetti. The Meal B (Dinner) bundle is designed for two diners and typically includes two pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken (deep-fried, crispy-coated, juicy interior), one Yumburger (beef patty in a soft white bun with condiments), a serving of Jolly Crispy Fries or steamed rice, and two regular soft drinks. The meal is Halal-certified at Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense, high-sodium, high-fat meal set. Per 100 g, the composite provides approximately 218 kcal, 9.2 g protein, 9.8 g fat (2.9 g saturated), and 24.5 g carbohydrate. The full set for two persons is estimated to deliver approximately 2180 kcal total, making it a high-energy shared meal. The glycemic load per person per sitting is estimated at approximately 30 (medium-to-high), driven by white rice, burger bun, fries, and sugar-sweetened beverages. Clinically, this meal is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load from refined carbohydrates and sugary drinks), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium). It is not suitable for calorie-restricted or carbohydrate-restricted dietary plans. The meal is best consumed occasionally and is not recommended for individuals with chronic metabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: The exact composition of the Value for Two Meal B (Dinner) bundle at Jollibee Singapore Bugis Village is not published in any primary nutrition database. Nutrient values are estimated from a representative composite of Jollibee Philippines official nutrition data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the individual components (fried chicken, burger, fries, rice, soft drink). The bundle composition is inferred from Jollibee's standard Meal B dinner offering. Confidence is medium due to bundle composition uncertainty, Singapore-specific portion variation, and the absence of a direct Singapore-published nutrition disclosure for this specific bundle.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of Jollibee Philippines official nutrition data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries. The exact Singapore bundle composition for Meal B (Dinner) is not independently verified from a Singapore-specific primary source. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee Philippines nutrition disclosure or the USDA SR Legacy composite used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used for this estimation. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee Philippines nutrition disclosure or the USDA SR Legacy composite used for this estimation. 5. GI is an analogous estimate based on weighted composite of white rice (GI 72), burger bun (GI 70), and french fries (GI 63) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific bundle. 6. The meal is tagged Halal based on Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification status as listed on field survey data and consistent with Jollibee Singapore's publicly stated Halal status. 7. Serving size is estimated at 1000 g total for the full set for two persons; per-person portion is approximately 500 g. 8. All -1 sentinel values represent nutrients not reported in the source databases used for this composite estimation.
